title: The 10-Billion Peso Audit: Evaluating Spatial Weight Misspecification
text: ArchipelagoEngine solves optimizing parameter kk. k=5k=5, achieve singular connected component (nc=1nc=1), ensuring spatial multiplier effect can flow entire archipelago.
code: library(ArchipelagoEngine) library(spdep)  # Verification of Matrix Integration w_knn <- build_archipelago_weight(raw_data, k = 5) S_knn <- spdep::n.comp.nb(w_knn$neighbours)  print(S_knn$nc) # National Connectivity must equal 1 ## [1] 1

title: The 10-Billion Peso Audit: Evaluating Spatial Weight Misspecification
text: conclude Queen-contiguity invalid Philippine national policy analysis. Using fragmented WW matrix distribute 10 billion pesos appropriation results “geographic exclusion.” enforcing 100% connectivity, ensure spatial spillovers—resulting policy interventions—mathematically representative entire nation. Furthermore, transition disjoint Queen-matrix connected KNN-matrix ensures spectral radius WW well-behaved (ρ(W)=1\rho(W) = 1), allowing stable maximum likelihood estimation spatial autoregressive parameter λ\lambda. Specifically, ensuring single connected component (nc=1nc = 1), guarantee Jacobian term ln|−λW|\ln|- \lambda W| log-likelihood function monotonic well-defined feasible range λ\lambda.

what: Spatial Weight Construction for Archipelagic Geographies
title: Spatial Weight Construction for Archipelagic Geographies
text: Standard spatial contiguity models often leave significant portions island nations mathematically isolated. Philippine context, standard Queen logic leaves 16 provinces (approx. 20%) orphaned, resulting fragmented network 80.2% connectivity. fragmentation introduces systematic predictive bias significant residual spatial autocorrelation (e.g., Moran’s =0.024I=0.024, p<0.05p<0.05 ‘palay’ price Philippines). ArchipelagoEngine implements specialized K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) logic bridge fragmented maritime networks. enforcing unified grid (optimized k=5k=5 using Philippines case study), engine achieves 100% network connectivity neutralizes spatial bias, enabling robust econometric inference fragmented topographies, public health mapping, among applications.

title: Spatial Weight Construction for Archipelagic Geographies
text: Figure 1: Standard Queen Logic (Left) vs. ArchipelagoEngine k=5 (Right) 100% Connectivity: Ensures island units mathematically “orphaned.” Bias Neutralization: Reduces Moran’s approximately 0 (p>0.10p > 0.10) stabilize spatial spillovers. Structural Robustness: Prioritizes structural integrity randomized residuals superficial model fit. CRAN Verified: Fully compatible standard spatial regression workflows using sf spdep.

title: Spatial Weight Construction for Archipelagic Geographies
text: can install released version ArchipelagoEngine CRAN :

title: Spatial Weight Construction for Archipelagic Geographies
text: core function, build_archipelago_weight, bridges fragmented networks using optimized KNN logic.
code: library(ArchipelagoEngine) library(sf) library(spdep)  # Load the benchmark map data(raw_data)  # Calculate the new network based on the k=5 logic weights <- build_archipelago_weight(raw_data, k = 5)  # Scans the graph to see if any islands are isolated # If this returns 1, the computer has verified 100% connectivity connectivity_status <- spdep::n.comp.nb(weights$neighbours)$nc print(connectivity_status)  # Plot connectivity plot(st_geometry(raw_data), border = "lightgrey") plot(weights$neighbours, st_coordinates(st_centroid(raw_data)),       add = TRUE, col = "#1E90FF", pch = 19, cex = 0.5, lwd = 0.7)  # Result status mtext(paste("Status: 100% Connectivity Achieved (nc =", connectivity_status, ")"),        side = 1, line = 1, adj = 0.5, cex = 0.9, font = 1, col = "#2C3E50")

title: Build Archipelagic Spatial Weights — build_archipelago_weight

title: Build Archipelagic Spatial Weights — build_archipelago_weight
Build Archipelagic Spatial Weights — build_archipelago_weight
text: p_map sf object containing geographic boundaries. k Integer. Number neighbors. Default 5, optimized Philippine archipelagic connectivity.

title: Build Archipelagic Spatial Weights — build_archipelago_weight
text: listw object compatible spatial regression models.

title: Build Archipelagic Spatial Weights — build_archipelago_weight
text: Standard Queen-contiguity models inherently fail archipelagic settings. Philippine context, Queen logic leaves 16 provinces (approx. 20%) mathematically isolated, resulting fragmented network 80.2% connectivity. fragmentation introduces systematic predictive bias, evidenced significant Residual Spatial Autocorrelation (Moran's = 0.024, p < 0.05) higher AIC (201.896). enforcing unified grid (k=5), function achieves: 100% Network Connectivity (nc=1) Neutralized Spatial Bias (Moran's approx. 0, p > 0.10) Robust Spatial Spillovers (Lambda stable ~0.26) Queen model may appear "tighter" fit (Log-Likelihood: -96.948), KNN (k=5) specification (Log-Likelihood: -97.472) prioritized structural robustness randomized residuals.

title: Build Archipelagic Spatial Weights — build_archipelago_weight
code: # \donttest{   # Example: Ensuring 100% connectivity for 81 provinces   weights <- build_archipelago_weight(raw_data, k = 5)   spdep::n.comp.nb(weights$neighbours)$nc #> [1] 1 # }
